name: Application Shimming File Access or Modification Detected
description: Detects suspicious file accesses or modifications to Application Shimming database files (`.sdb`) and associated registry keys, or the execution of `sdbinst.exe`. Adversaries can leverage Windows Application Shimming for persistence, privilege escalation, and defense evasion by installing custom shim databases that can redirect execution or inject DLLs into processes.

prompts.prompt: A critical security event has been detected on the host **{computer_name}**: suspicious activity related to **Application Shimming** (**MITRE ATT&CK T1546.011**) has occurred. The process **{image}**, running under user **{user}**, either executed `sdbinst.exe` with the command line **{command_line}**, or a malicious shim database file (`.sdb`) was created or modified at **{target_filename}**, or a related registry key was modified at **{target_object}**. This technique is a known method for achieving **persistence**, **privilege escalation**, and **defense evasion**. The use of Application Shimming for non-legitimate purposes is highly malicious. Immediate investigation is required to determine the purpose of this activity. Treat this as a confirmed security incident until proven otherwise.

parameters.name: image
parameters.description: The process that performed the activity, if a process creation event was detected.

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The command line of the sdbinst.exe process.

parameters.name: target_filename
parameters.description: The path to the SDB file that was created or modified.

parameters.name: target_object
parameters.description: The registry key that was modified.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which the activity occurred.

parameters.name: computer_name
parameters.description: The name of the computer where the event occurred.
